importance_sampling_1000.description: IS with 1000 particles, same model structure

linreg.note: GenMLX VIS is vectorized (1000 particles in one MLX graph)

gmm.note: GenMLX VIS-GMM skipped (mx/item in model body); using previous run data

hmm.note: HMM vectorized IS. Gen.jl is sequential

particle_scaling.note: GenMLX: 10x particles costs only 7% more time. JAX: 2x.

mcmc_5000_steps.description: MH with 5000 steps on linear regression
mcmc_5000_steps.note: Gen.jl's compiled Julia runtime vs GenMLX's nbb interpreter. MCMC is inherently sequential — each step depends on the previous.

smc_hmm.note: GenMLX batched SMC leverages Metal GPU parallelism

hmc_funnel.note: HMC requires sequential leapfrog steps — interpreter overhead dominates

unique_genmlx_features.note: Features unique to GenMLX — no Gen.jl or JAX equivalent

genmlx_wins: Vectorized IS: 1-6x faster than Gen.jl for parallel particle methods
Batched SMC: 2x faster than Gen.jl (Metal GPU parallelism)
Sublinear particle scaling: 10x particles costs 7% more time
Unique L3/L4 features: auto-conjugacy, compiled optimization, method selection
genjl_wins: MCMC (MH/HMC): 6-19x faster (compiled Julia vs interpreted ClojureScript)
Sequential operations benefit from Julia's compiled runtime
jax_wins: Bare JIT computation: 3x faster IS (XLA JIT vs Metal lazy graph)
summary.key_insight: GenMLX wins where parallelism matters (IS, SMC), loses where sequential steps dominate (MCMC). The compilation ladder progressively closes the gap.
